{{nihongo|{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|12=draft}}|[[:ja:天皇の杜古墳|天皇の杜古墳]]}} is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Misasagi Tsukanokoshi-chō, Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|京都市西京区御陵塚ノ越町|12=draft}}, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} (as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}}). == Overview == This tumulus was constructed along the western edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=draft}}, near the foothills on the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River (Yodo River system)|ja|桂川|12=draft}}. Since the medieval period, the mound has been traditionally associated with {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇|12=draft}}, the 55th Emperor of Japan from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}. The name "Tenno-no-mori" (lit. "Emperor's Forest") and the surrounding area name "Misasagi" (imperial mausoleum) are said to derive from this tradition. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} The mound remains well-preserved, and excavations were conducted between {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|12=draft}} and {{ill|1989|ja|1989th year|12=draft}}.Site explanation board. The tumulus has a distinctive "mirror-handle" keyhole shape with an unexpanded front portion facing south-southeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} Outer surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (cylindrical and morning glory-shaped). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} There is no surrounding moat, but remnants of a demarcated area resembling a moat have been identified. The burial facility has not been investigated. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} during the early {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=draft}}. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} Archaeological evidence suggests the individual buried here was a prominent figure who controlled the Katsura River's right bank. During this period, the transition from keyhole-shaped to circular mounds is evident in the {{ill|Mukō Hills|ja|向日丘陵|12=draft}} tombs such as {{ill|Myōkenyama Kofun (Mukō)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Den-Takabatake Tomb|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=draft}}. However, this site stands out as a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, indicating political importance surpassing the Mukō Hills. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|12=draft}}, and it has been developed into a public historical park. == Site History == * '''1922 (Taishō 11)''': Designated as a National Historic Site.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * '''1981-1982 (Shōwa 56-57)''': Public ownership of the surrounding area was established. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * '''1988-1989 (Shōwa 63-Heisei 1)''': Excavations were conducted. * '''1994 (Heisei 6)''': Opening of the historical park. * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': The National Historic Site "Tenno-no-mori Kofun" was integrated with other tumuli such as {{ill|Egeyama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun|12=draft}} and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft}} into the expanded designation "Otokuni Kofun Cluster." Eight additional tumuli were included in this designation.March 1, 2016, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 31. [http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, press release, November 20, 2015). == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] (Kyoto City Information Center). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * Total length: 83.0 meters * Posterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 50.5 meters ''' Height: 7.2 meters * Anterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 33.5 meters ''' Height: 4.8 meters Tenno-no-mori Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Posterior mound top Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from posterior to anterior mound 天皇の杜古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Kyoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|京都市考古資料館|12=draft}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Tenno-no-mori Kofun''' (as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster) **''' Designated as a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922, with an area of 10,234 square meters. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} **''' Integrated with other tumuli in 2016 to form the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster" designation. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site explanation board {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天皇の杜古墳 |別名 = 天皇ノ杜古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所属 = 乙訓古墳群 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[西京区]]御陵塚ノ越町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 58|緯度秒 = 39.04 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 41|経度秒 = 22.74 |ISO = JP-26 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]]（柄鏡式） |規模 = 墳丘長83.0m
高さ7.2m（後円部） |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]末葉 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪 |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「天皇の杜古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含） |特記事項 = |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=22.74|label=天皇の杜
古墳|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''天皇の杜古墳'''（てんのうのもりこふん、天皇ノ杜古墳{{Sfn|天皇ノ杜古墳（古墳）|1989}}）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[西京区]]御陵塚ノ越町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == [[京都盆地]]西縁、[[桂川 (淀川水系)|桂川]]右岸の山裾に築造された古墳である。[[中世]]期から[[文徳天皇]]（[[平安時代]]の第55代天皇）の陵と伝承されており{{Sfn|天皇の杜古墳（平凡社）|1979}}、古墳名の「天皇の杜」や周辺地名の「御陵」はそれに由来するという{{Sfn|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}}。現在までに墳丘を良好に遺存し、[[1988年|1988]]-[[1989年]]度（[[昭和]]63-[[平成]]元年度）には発掘調査が実施されている史跡説明板。。 墳形は前方部が広がらない柄鏡式の前方後円形で、前方部を南南東方向に向ける{{Sfn|天皇ノ杜古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]列（[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪）が検出されている{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。墳丘周囲に周濠は巡らされていないが、周濠様の兆域区画が認められている。埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末頃と推定される{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、考古学的には桂川右岸を治めた有力者と見られる{{Sfn|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}}。桂川流域では、向日丘陵の首長墳が[[妙見山古墳 (向日市)|妙見山古墳]]から[[伝高畠陵古墳]]（いずれも[[向日市]]）において前方後円墳から円墳に変化する一方、当地域の本古墳は大型前方後円墳として向日丘陵よりも優位性を示しており、一帯の政治情勢を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定され、現在は史跡公園として整備・開放されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）3月8日、「天皇の杜古墳」として国の[[史跡]]に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1638|乙訓古墳群}}。 * [[1981年|1981]]-[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]56-57年）、墳丘周囲部を公有地化{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。 * [[1988年|1988]]-[[1989年]]度（昭和63-[[平成]]元年度）、発掘調査。 * [[1994年]]（平成6年）4月、史跡公園の開園。 * [[2016年]]（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の国の史跡「天皇の杜古墳」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「[[寺戸大塚古墳]]」の3件を統合し、これに従来未指定であった古墳8基を追加指定して、史跡指定名称を「乙訓古墳群」に変更平成28年3月1日文部科学省告示第31号[https://web.archive.org/web/20160512004751/http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html 「史跡等の指定等について」]（文化庁記者発表、2015年11月20日）。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html 天皇の杜古墳]（京都市ホームページ「京都市情報館」）。{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。 * 墳丘長：83.0メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 幅：50.5メートル ** 高さ：7.2メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 幅：33.5メートル ** 高さ：4.8メートル Tenno-no-mori Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|後円部墳頂 Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む 天皇の杜古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|円筒埴輪
{{small|[[京都市考古資料館]]企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 天皇の杜古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 1922年（大正11年）3月8日に「天皇の杜古墳」として国の史跡に指定（指定範囲面積は10,234平方メートル）{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。 *: 2016年（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の史跡「天皇の杜古墳」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「[[寺戸大塚古墳]]」の3件を統合し、これに[[五塚原古墳]]など8基の古墳を追加指定し、指定名称を「乙訓古墳群」に変更。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（京都市文化財保護課設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|title=史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|publisher=京都市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=天皇ノ杜古墳|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kyotofu-maibun.or.jp/data/kankou/kankou-pdf/jyouhou/kyoutofu-J71.pdf 京都府埋蔵文化財情報 第71号]}}|publisher=京都府埋蔵文化財調査研究センター|isbn=|pages=45-46|ref={{Harvid|京都府埋蔵文化財情報 第71号|1999}}}} - リンクは京都府埋蔵文化財調査研究センター。 * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=天皇の杜古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|天皇の杜古墳（平凡社）|1979}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=和田晴吾|authorlink=和田晴吾|year=1989|chapter=天皇ノ杜古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|天皇ノ杜古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1444522 天皇の杜古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tenno-no-mori Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1638|乙訓古墳群}} * [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html 天皇の杜古墳] - 京都市ホームページ「京都市情報館」 {{DEFAULTSORT:てんのうのもりこふん}} [[Category:乙訓古墳群]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:西京区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|"
revision.text:
{{nihongo|{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}|[[:ja:天皇の杜古墳|天皇の杜古墳]]}} is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Misasagi Tsukanokoshi-chō, Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|京都市西京区御陵塚ノ越町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} (as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Overview == This tumulus was constructed along the western edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, near the foothills on the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River (Yodo River system)|ja|桂川|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since the medieval period, the mound has been traditionally associated with {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, the 55th Emperor of Japan from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The name "Tenno-no-mori" (lit. "Emperor's Forest") and the surrounding area name "Misasagi" (imperial mausoleum) are said to derive from this tradition. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} The mound remains well-preserved, and excavations were conducted between {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|1989|ja|1989th year|12=draft|12=draft}}.Site explanation board. The tumulus has a distinctive "mirror-handle" keyhole shape with an unexpanded front portion facing south-southeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} Outer surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} (cylindrical and morning glory-shaped). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} There is no surrounding moat, but remnants of a demarcated area resembling a moat have been identified. The burial facility has not been investigated. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the early {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=draft|12=draft}}. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} Archaeological evidence suggests the individual buried here was a prominent figure who controlled the Katsura River's right bank. During this period, the transition from keyhole-shaped to circular mounds is evident in the {{ill|Mukō Hills|ja|向日丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} tombs such as {{ill|Myōkenyama Kofun (Mukō)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Den-Takabatake Tomb|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, this site stands out as a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, indicating political importance surpassing the Mukō Hills. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it has been developed into a public historical park. == Site History == * '''1922 (Taishō 11)''': Designated as a National Historic Site.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * '''1981-1982 (Shōwa 56-57)''': Public ownership of the surrounding area was established. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * '''1988-1989 (Shōwa 63-Heisei 1)''': Excavations were conducted. * '''1994 (Heisei 6)''': Opening of the historical park. * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': The National Historic Site "Tenno-no-mori Kofun" was integrated with other tumuli such as {{ill|Egeyama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} into the expanded designation "Otokuni Kofun Cluster." Eight additional tumuli were included in this designation.March 1, 2016, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 31. [http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, press release, November 20, 2015). == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] (Kyoto City Information Center). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * Total length: 83.0 meters * Posterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 50.5 meters ''' Height: 7.2 meters * Anterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 33.5 meters ''' Height: 4.8 meters Tenno-no-mori Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Posterior mound top Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from posterior to anterior mound 天皇の杜古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Kyoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|京都市考古資料館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Tenno-no-mori Kofun''' (as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster) **''' Designated as a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922, with an area of 10,234 square meters. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} **''' Integrated with other tumuli in 2016 to form the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster" designation. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site explanation board (Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Section) * Publications by local governments ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|title=Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|publisher=Kyoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kyotofu-maibun.or.jp/data/kankou/kankou-pdf/jyouhou/kyoutofu-J71.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Information, Issue 71]}}|publisher=Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Research Center|isbn=|pages=45-46|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Information, Issue 71|1999}}}} * Other references ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 27 Kyoto City|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Haruo Wada|year=1989|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1444522 Tenno-no-mori Kofun]|title=National Historic Site Guide|publisher=[[Kodansha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}} - Link to Asahi Shimbun "Kotobank." == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tenno-no-mori Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] - Kyoto City Information Center [[JA:%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nishikyō-ku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun]] [[Category:伝高畠陵古墳]] }} [[sn:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun]] [[ja:天皇の杜古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}

[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, press release, November 20, 2015). == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] (Kyoto City Information Center). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * Total length: 83.0 meters * Posterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 50.5 meters ''' Height: 7.2 meters * Anterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 33.5 meters ''' Height: 4.8 meters Tenno-no-mori Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Posterior mound top Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from posterior to anterior mound 天皇の杜古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Kofun''' (as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster) **''' Designated as a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922, with an area of 10,234 square meters. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} **''' Integrated with other tumuli in 2016 to form the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster" designation. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site explanation board (Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Section) * Publications by local governments ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|title=Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|publisher=Kyoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kyotofu-maibun.or.jp/data/kankou/kankou-pdf/jyouhou/kyoutofu-J71.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Information, Issue 71]}}|publisher=Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Research Center|isbn=|pages=45-46|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Information, Issue 71|1999}}}} * Other references ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 27 Kyoto City|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Haruo Wada|year=1989|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1444522 Tenno-no-mori Kofun]|title=National Historic Site Guide|publisher=[[Kodansha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}} - Link to Asahi Shimbun "Kotobank." == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tenno-no-mori Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] - Kyoto City Information Center [[JA:%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nishikyō-ku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun]] [[Category:伝高畠陵古墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun]] [[ja:天皇の杜古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}

{{nihongo|{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|[[:ja:天皇の杜古墳|天皇の杜古墳]]}} is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Misasagi Tsukanokoshi-chō, Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|京都市西京区御陵塚ノ越町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}} (as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Overview == This tumulus was constructed along the western edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, near the foothills on the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River (Yodo River system)|ja|桂川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since the medieval period, the mound has been traditionally associated with {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the 55th Emperor of Japan from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The name "Tenno-no-mori" (lit. "Emperor's Forest") and the surrounding area name "Misasagi" (imperial mausoleum) are said to derive from this tradition. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} The mound remains well-preserved, and excavations were conducted between {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|1989|ja|1989th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Site explanation board. The tumulus has a distinctive "mirror-handle" keyhole shape with an unexpanded front portion facing south-southeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} Outer surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (cylindrical and morning glory-shaped). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} There is no surrounding moat, but remnants of a demarcated area resembling a moat have been identified. The burial facility has not been investigated. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the early {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} Archaeological evidence suggests the individual buried here was a prominent figure who controlled the Katsura River's right bank. During this period, the transition from keyhole-shaped to circular mounds is evident in the {{ill|Mukō Hills|ja|向日丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} tombs such as {{ill|Myōkenyama Kofun (Mukō)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Den-Takabatake Tomb|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, this site stands out as a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, indicating political importance surpassing the Mukō Hills. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it has been developed into a public historical park. == Site History == * '''1922 (Taishō 11)''': Designated as a National Historic Site.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * '''1981-1982 (Shōwa 56-57)''': Public ownership of the surrounding area was established. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * '''1988-1989 (Shōwa 63-Heisei 1)''': Excavations were conducted. * '''1994 (Heisei 6)''': Opening of the historical park. * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': The National Historic Site "Tenno-no-mori Kofun" was integrated with other tumuli such as {{ill|Egeyama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into the expanded designation "Otokuni Kofun Cluster." Eight additional tumuli were included in this designation.March 1, 2016, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 31. [http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, press release, November 20, 2015). == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] (Kyoto City Information Center). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * Total length: 83.0 meters * Posterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 50.5 meters ''' Height: 7.2 meters * Anterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 33.5 meters ''' Height: 4.8 meters Tenno-no-mori Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Posterior mound top Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from posterior to anterior mound 天皇の杜古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Kyoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|京都市考古資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Tenno-no-mori Kofun''' (as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster) **''' Designated as a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922, with an area of 10,234 square meters. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} **''' Integrated with other tumuli in 2016 to form the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster" designation. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site explanation board Kofun|publisher=Kyoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=Tenno-no-mori revision.comment: 28 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:天皇の杜古墳 up to rev 104551982
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 天皇の杜古墳 |別名 = 天皇ノ杜古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所属 = 乙訓古墳群 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[西京区]]御陵塚ノ越町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 58|緯度秒 = 39.04 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 41|経度秒 = 22.74 |ISO = JP-26 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]]（柄鏡式） |規模 = 墳丘長83.0m
高さ7.2m（後円部） |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]末葉 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪 |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「天皇の杜古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含） |特記事項 = |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=22.74|label=天皇の杜
古墳|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''天皇の杜古墳'''（てんのうのもりこふん、天皇ノ杜古墳{{Sfn|天皇ノ杜古墳（古墳）|1989}}）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[西京区]]御陵塚ノ越町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == [[京都盆地]]西縁、[[桂川 (淀川水系)|桂川]]右岸の山裾に築造された古墳である。[[中世]]期から[[文徳天皇]]（[[平安時代]]の第55代天皇）の陵と伝承されており{{Sfn|天皇の杜古墳（平凡社）|1979}}、古墳名の「天皇の杜」や周辺地名の「御陵」はそれに由来するという{{Sfn|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}}。現在までに墳丘を良好に遺存し、[[1988年|1988]]-[[1989年]]度（[[昭和]]63-[[平成]]元年度）には発掘調査が実施されている史跡説明板。。 墳形は前方部が広がらない柄鏡式の前方後円形で、前方部を南南東方向に向ける{{Sfn|天皇ノ杜古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]列（[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪）が検出されている{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。墳丘周囲に周濠は巡らされていないが、周濠様の兆域区画が認められている。埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末頃と推定される{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、考古学的には桂川右岸を治めた有力者と見られる{{Sfn|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}}。桂川流域では、向日丘陵の首長墳が[[妙見山古墳 (向日市)|妙見山古墳]]から[[伝高畠陵古墳]]（いずれも[[向日市]]）において前方後円墳から円墳に変化する一方、当地域の本古墳は大型前方後円墳として向日丘陵よりも優位性を示しており、一帯の政治情勢を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定され、現在は史跡公園として整備・開放されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）3月8日、「天皇の杜古墳」として国の[[史跡]]に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1638|乙訓古墳群}}。 * [[1981年|1981]]-[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]56-57年）、墳丘周囲部を公有地化{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。 * [[1988年|1988]]-[[1989年]]度（昭和63-[[平成]]元年度）、発掘調査。 * [[1994年]]（平成6年）4月、史跡公園の開園。 * [[2016年]]（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の国の史跡「天皇の杜古墳」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「[[寺戸大塚古墳]]」の3件を統合し、これに従来未指定であった古墳8基を追加指定して、史跡指定名称を「乙訓古墳群」に変更平成28年3月1日文部科学省告示第31号[https://web.archive.org/web/20160512004751/http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html 「史跡等の指定等について」]（文化庁記者発表、2015年11月20日）。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html 天皇の杜古墳]（京都市ホームページ「京都市情報館」）。{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。 * 墳丘長：83.0メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 幅：50.5メートル ** 高さ：7.2メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 幅：33.5メートル ** 高さ：4.8メートル Tenno-no-mori Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|後円部墳頂 Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む 天皇の杜古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|円筒埴輪
{{small|[[京都市考古資料館]]企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 天皇の杜古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 1922年（大正11年）3月8日に「天皇の杜古墳」として国の史跡に指定（指定範囲面積は10,234平方メートル）{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。 *: 2016年（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の史跡「天皇の杜古墳」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「[[寺戸大塚古墳]]」の3件を統合し、これに[[五塚原古墳]]など8基の古墳を追加指定し、指定名称を「乙訓古墳群」に変更。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（京都市文化財保護課設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|title=史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|publisher=京都市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=天皇ノ杜古墳|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kyotofu-maibun.or.jp/data/kankou/kankou-pdf/jyouhou/kyoutofu-J71.pdf 京都府埋蔵文化財情報 第71号]}}|publisher=京都府埋蔵文化財調査研究センター|isbn=|pages=45-46|ref={{Harvid|京都府埋蔵文化財情報 第71号|1999}}}} - リンクは京都府埋蔵文化財調査研究センター。 * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=天皇の杜古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|天皇の杜古墳（平凡社）|1979}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=和田晴吾|authorlink=和田晴吾|year=1989|chapter=天皇ノ杜古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|天皇ノ杜古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1444522 天皇の杜古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tenno-no-mori Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1638|乙訓古墳群}} * [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html 天皇の杜古墳] - 京都市ホームページ「京都市情報館」 {{DEFAULTSORT:てんのうのもりこふん}} [[Category:乙訓古墳群]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:西京区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

高さ7.2m（後円部） |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]末葉 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪 |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「天皇の杜古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含） |特記事項 = |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=22.74|label=天皇の杜
古墳|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''天皇の杜古墳'''（てんのうのもりこふん、天皇ノ杜古墳{{Sfn|天皇ノ杜古墳（古墳）|1989}}）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[西京区]]御陵塚ノ越町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == [[京都盆地]]西縁、[[桂川 (淀川水系)|桂川]]右岸の山裾に築造された古墳である。[[中世]]期から[[文徳天皇]]（[[平安時代]]の第55代天皇）の陵と伝承されており{{Sfn|天皇の杜古墳（平凡社）|1979}}、古墳名の「天皇の杜」や周辺地名の「御陵」はそれに由来するという{{Sfn|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}}。現在までに墳丘を良好に遺存し、[[1988年|1988]]-[[1989年]]度（[[昭和]]63-[[平成]]元年度）には発掘調査が実施されている史跡説明板。。 墳形は前方部が広がらない柄鏡式の前方後円形で、前方部を南南東方向に向ける{{Sfn|天皇ノ杜古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]列（[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪）が検出されている{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。墳丘周囲に周濠は巡らされていないが、周濠様の兆域区画が認められている。埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末頃と推定される{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、考古学的には桂川右岸を治めた有力者と見られる{{Sfn|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}}。桂川流域では、向日丘陵の首長墳が[[妙見山古墳 (向日市)|妙見山古墳]]から[[伝高畠陵古墳]]（いずれも[[向日市]]）において前方後円墳から円墳に変化する一方、当地域の本古墳は大型前方後円墳として向日丘陵よりも優位性を示しており、一帯の政治情勢を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定され、現在は史跡公園として整備・開放されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）3月8日、「天皇の杜古墳」として国の[[史跡]]に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1638|乙訓古墳群}}。 * [[1981年|1981]]-[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]56-57年）、墳丘周囲部を公有地化{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。 * [[1988年|1988]]-[[1989年]]度（昭和63-[[平成]]元年度）、発掘調査。 * [[1994年]]（平成6年）4月、史跡公園の開園。 * [[2016年]]（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の国の史跡「天皇の杜古墳」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「[[寺戸大塚古墳]]」の3件を統合し、これに従来未指定であった古墳8基を追加指定して、史跡指定名称を「乙訓古墳群」に変更平成28年3月1日文部科学省告示第31号[https://web.archive.org/web/20160512004751/http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html 「史跡等の指定等について」]（文化庁記者発表、2015年11月20日）。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html 天皇の杜古墳]（京都市ホームページ「京都市情報館」）。{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。 * 墳丘長：83.0メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 幅：50.5メートル ** 高さ：7.2メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 幅：33.5メートル ** 高さ：4.8メートル Tenno-no-mori Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|後円部墳頂 Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む 天皇の杜古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|円筒埴輪
{{small|[[京都市考古資料館]]企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 天皇の杜古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 1922年（大正11年）3月8日に「天皇の杜古墳」として国の史跡に指定（指定範囲面積は10,234平方メートル）{{Sfn|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994|pp=5-11}}。 *: 2016年（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の史跡「天皇の杜古墳」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「[[寺戸大塚古墳]]」の3件を統合し、これに[[五塚原古墳]]など8基の古墳を追加指定し、指定名称を「乙訓古墳群」に変更。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（京都市文化財保護課設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|title=史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|publisher=京都市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史跡天皇の杜古墳 保存整備事業報告|1994}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=天皇ノ杜古墳|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kyotofu-maibun.or.jp/data/kankou/kankou-pdf/jyouhou/kyoutofu-J71.pdf 京都府埋蔵文化財情報 第71号]}}|publisher=京都府埋蔵文化財調査研究センター|isbn=|pages=45-46|ref={{Harvid|京都府埋蔵文化財情報 第71号|1999}}}} - リンクは京都府埋蔵文化財調査研究センター。 * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=天皇の杜古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|天皇の杜古墳（平凡社）|1979}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=和田晴吾|authorlink=和田晴吾|year=1989|chapter=天皇ノ杜古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|天皇ノ杜古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1444522 天皇の杜古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tenno-no-mori Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1638|乙訓古墳群}} * [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html 天皇の杜古墳] - 京都市ホームページ「京都市情報館」 {{DEFAULTSORT:てんのうのもりこふん}} [[Category:乙訓古墳群]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:西京区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

{{nihongo|{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|[[:ja:天皇の杜古墳|天皇の杜古墳]]}} is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Misasagi Tsukanokoshi-chō, Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|京都市西京区御陵塚ノ越町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}} (as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Overview == This tumulus was constructed along the western edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, near the foothills on the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River (Yodo River system)|ja|桂川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since the medieval period, the mound has been traditionally associated with {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the 55th Emperor of Japan from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The name "Tenno-no-mori" (lit. "Emperor's Forest") and the surrounding area name "Misasagi" (imperial mausoleum) are said to derive from this tradition. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} The mound remains well-preserved, and excavations were conducted between {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|1989|ja|1989th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Site explanation board. The tumulus has a distinctive "mirror-handle" keyhole shape with an unexpanded front portion facing south-southeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} Outer surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (cylindrical and morning glory-shaped). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} There is no surrounding moat, but remnants of a demarcated area resembling a moat have been identified. The burial facility has not been investigated. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the early {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} Archaeological evidence suggests the individual buried here was a prominent figure who controlled the Katsura River's right bank. During this period, the transition from keyhole-shaped to circular mounds is evident in the {{ill|Mukō Hills|ja|向日丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} tombs such as {{ill|Myōkenyama Kofun (Mukō)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Den-Takabatake Tomb|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, this site stands out as a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, indicating political importance surpassing the Mukō Hills. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it has been developed into a public historical park. == Site History == * '''1922 (Taishō 11)''': Designated as a National Historic Site.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * '''1981-1982 (Shōwa 56-57)''': Public ownership of the surrounding area was established. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * '''1988-1989 (Shōwa 63-Heisei 1)''': Excavations were conducted. * '''1994 (Heisei 6)''': Opening of the historical park. * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': The National Historic Site "Tenno-no-mori Kofun" was integrated with other tumuli such as {{ill|Egeyama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into the expanded designation "Otokuni Kofun Cluster." Eight additional tumuli were included in this designation.March 1, 2016, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 31. [http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, press release, November 20, 2015). == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] (Kyoto City Information Center). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * Total length: 83.0 meters * Posterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 50.5 meters ''' Height: 7.2 meters * Anterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 33.5 meters ''' Height: 4.8 meters Tenno-no-mori Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Posterior mound top Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from posterior to anterior mound 天皇の杜古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} **''' Integrated with other tumuli in 2016 to form the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster" designation. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site explanation board {{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Kyoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|京都市考古資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Tenno-no-mori Kofun''' (as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster) **''' Designated as a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922, with an area of 10,234 square meters. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} **''' Integrated with other tumuli in 2016 to form the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster" designation. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site explanation board (Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Section) * Publications by local governments ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|title=Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|publisher=Kyoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kyotofu-maibun.or.jp/data/kankou/kankou-pdf/jyouhou/kyoutofu-J71.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Information, Issue 71]}}|publisher=Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Research Center|isbn=|pages=45-46|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Information, Issue 71|1999}}}} * Other references ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 27 Kyoto City|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Haruo Wada|year=1989|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1444522 Tenno-no-mori Kofun]|title=National Historic Site Guide|publisher=[[Kodansha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}} - Link to Asahi Shimbun "Kotobank." == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tenno-no-mori Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] - Kyoto City Information Center [[JA:%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|天皇の杜古墳|version=104551982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nishikyō-ku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun]] [[Category:伝高畠陵古墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun]] [[ja:天皇の杜古墳]]

{{nihongo|{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|[[:ja:天皇の杜古墳|天皇の杜古墳]]}} is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Misasagi Tsukanokoshi-chō, Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|京都市西京区御陵塚ノ越町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}} (as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Overview == This tumulus was constructed along the western edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, near the foothills on the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River (Yodo River system)|ja|桂川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since the medieval period, the mound has been traditionally associated with {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the 55th Emperor of Japan from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The name "Tenno-no-mori" (lit. "Emperor's Forest") and the surrounding area name "Misasagi" (imperial mausoleum) are said to derive from this tradition. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} The mound remains well-preserved, and excavations were conducted between {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|1989|ja|1989th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Site explanation board. The tumulus has a distinctive "mirror-handle" keyhole shape with an unexpanded front portion facing south-southeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} Outer surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (cylindrical and morning glory-shaped). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} There is no surrounding moat, but remnants of a demarcated area resembling a moat have been identified. The burial facility has not been investigated. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the early {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} Archaeological evidence suggests the individual buried here was a prominent figure who controlled the Katsura River's right bank. During this period, the transition from keyhole-shaped to circular mounds is evident in the {{ill|Mukō Hills|ja|向日丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} tombs such as {{ill|Myōkenyama Kofun (Mukō)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Den-Takabatake Tomb|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, this site stands out as a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, indicating political importance surpassing the Mukō Hills. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it has been developed into a public historical park. == Site History == * '''1922 (Taishō 11)''': Designated as a National Historic Site.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * '''1981-1982 (Shōwa 56-57)''': Public ownership of the surrounding area was established. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * '''1988-1989 (Shōwa 63-Heisei 1)''': Excavations were conducted. * '''1994 (Heisei 6)''': Opening of the historical park. * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': The National Historic Site "Tenno-no-mori Kofun" was integrated with other tumuli such as {{ill|Egeyama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into the expanded designation "Otokuni Kofun Cluster." Eight additional tumuli were included in this designation.March 1, 2016, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 31. [http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, press release, November 20, 2015). == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] (Kyoto City Information Center). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * Total length: 83.0 meters * Posterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 50.5 meters ''' Height: 7.2 meters * Anterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 33.5 meters ''' Height: 4.8 meters Tenno-no-mori Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Posterior mound top Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from posterior to anterior mound 天皇の杜古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Kyoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|京都市考古資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Tenno-no-mori Kofun''' (as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster) **''' Designated as a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922, with an area of 10,234 square meters. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} **''' Integrated with other tumuli in 2016 to form the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster" designation. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site explanation board (Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Section) * Publications by local governments ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|title=Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|publisher=Kyoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kyotofu-maibun.or.jp/data/kankou/kankou-pdf/jyouhou/kyoutofu-J71.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Information, Issue 71]}}|publisher=Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Research Center|isbn=|pages=45-46|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Information, Issue 71|1999}}}} * Other references ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 27 Kyoto City|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Haruo Wada|year=1989|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1444522 Tenno-no-mori Kofun]|title=National Historic Site Guide|publisher=[[Kodansha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}} - Link to Asahi Shimbun "Kotobank." == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tenno-no-mori Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] - Kyoto City Information Center During this period, the transition from keyhole-shaped to circular mounds is evident in the {{ill|Mukō Hills|ja|向日丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} tombs such as {{ill|Myōkenyama Kofun (Mukō)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Den-Takabatake Tomb|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, this site stands out as a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, indicating political importance surpassing the Mukō Hills. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it has been developed into a public historical park. == Site History == * '''1922 (Taishō 11)''': Designated as a National Historic Site.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * '''1981-1982 (Shōwa 56-57)''': Public ownership of the surrounding area was established. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * '''1988-1989 (Shōwa 63-Heisei 1)''': Excavations were conducted. * '''1994 (Heisei 6)''': Opening of the historical park. * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': The National Historic Site "Tenno-no-mori Kofun" was integrated with other tumuli such as {{ill|Egeyama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into the expanded designation "Otokuni Kofun Cluster." Eight additional tumuli were included in this designation.March 1, 2016, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 31. [http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, press release, November 20, 2015). == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] (Kyoto City Information Center). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * Total length: 83.0 meters * Posterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 50.5 meters ''' Height: 7.2 meters * Anterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 33.5 meters ''' Height: 4.8 meters Tenno-no-mori Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Posterior mound top Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from posterior to anterior mound 天皇の杜古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Kyoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|京都市考古資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Tenno-no-mori Kofun''' (as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster) **''' Designated as a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922, with an area of 10,234 square meters. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} **''' Integrated with other tumuli in 2016 to form the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster" designation. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site explanation board (Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Section) * Publications by local governments ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|title=Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|publisher=Kyoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kyotofu-maibun.or.jp/data/kankou/kankou-pdf/jyouhou/kyoutofu-J71.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Information, Issue 71]}}|publisher=Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Research Center|isbn=|pages=45-46|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Information, Issue 71|1999}}}} * Other references ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 27 Kyoto City|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Haruo Wada|year=1989|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1444522 Tenno-no-mori Kofun]|title=National Historic Site Guide|publisher=[[Kodansha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}} - Link to Asahi Shimbun "Kotobank." == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tenno-no-mori Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] - Kyoto City Information Center [[JA:%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{translated page|ja|天皇の杜古墳|version=104551982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nishikyō-ku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun]] [[sn:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun]] [[ja:天皇の杜古墳]]

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{nihongo|{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|qq=}}|[[:ja:天皇の杜古墳|天皇の杜古墳]]}} is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Misasagi Tsukanokoshi-chō, Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|京都市西京区御陵塚ノ越町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasagi Tsukanokoshi-chō, Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}} (as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|qq=}}). == Overview == This tumulus was constructed along the western edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|qq=}}, near the foothills on the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River (Yodo River system)|ja|桂川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura River (Yodo River system)|qq=}}. Since the medieval period, the mound has been traditionally associated with {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Montoku|qq=}}, the 55th Emperor of Japan from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}. The name "Tenno-no-mori" (lit. "Emperor's Forest") and the surrounding area name "Misasagi" (imperial mausoleum) are said to derive from this tradition. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} The mound remains well-preserved, and excavations were conducted between {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1988|qq=}} and {{ill|1989|ja|1989th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}}.Site explanation board. The tumulus has a distinctive "mirror-handle" keyhole shape with an unexpanded front portion facing south-southeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} Outer surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} and rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} (cylindrical and morning glory-shaped). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} There is no surrounding moat, but remnants of a demarcated area resembling a moat have been identified. The burial facility has not been investigated. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/4th century|qq=}} during the early {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|qq=}}. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} Archaeological evidence suggests the individual buried here was a prominent figure who controlled the Katsura River's right bank. During this period, the transition from keyhole-shaped to circular mounds is evident in the {{ill|Mukō Hills|ja|向日丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills|qq=}} tombs such as {{ill|Myōkenyama Kofun (Mukō)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōkenyama Kofun (Mukō)|qq=}} and {{ill|Den-Takabatake Tomb|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatake Tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}. However, this site stands out as a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, indicating political importance surpassing the Mukō Hills. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1922|qq=}}, and it has been developed into a public historical park. == Site History == * '''1922 (Taishō 11)''': Designated as a National Historic Site.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * '''1981-1982 (Shōwa 56-57)''': Public ownership of the surrounding area was established. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * '''1988-1989 (Shōwa 63-Heisei 1)''': Excavations were conducted. * '''1994 (Heisei 6)''': Opening of the historical park. * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': The National Historic Site "Tenno-no-mori Kofun" was integrated with other tumuli such as {{ill|Egeyama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egeyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Igenoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Otsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} into the expanded designation "Otokuni Kofun Cluster." Eight additional tumuli were included in this designation.March 1, 2016, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 31. [http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, press release, November 20, 2015). == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] (Kyoto City Information Center). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * Total length: 83.0 meters * Posterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 50.5 meters ''' Height: 7.2 meters * Anterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 33.5 meters ''' Height: 4.8 meters Tenno-no-mori Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Posterior mound top Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from posterior to anterior mound 天皇の杜古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Kyoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|京都市考古資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Archaeological Museum|qq=}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Tenno-no-mori Kofun''' (as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster) **''' Designated as a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922, with an area of 10,234 square meters. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} **''' Integrated with other tumuli in 2016 to form the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster" designation. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site explanation board (Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Section) * Publications by local governments ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|title=Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|publisher=Kyoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kyotofu-maibun.or.jp/data/kankou/kankou-pdf/jyouhou/kyoutofu-J71.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Information, Issue 71]}}|publisher=Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Research Center|isbn=|pages=45-46|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Information, Issue 71|1999}}}} * Other references ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 27 Kyoto City|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Haruo Wada|year=1989|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1444522 Tenno-no-mori Kofun]|title=National Historic Site Guide|publisher=[[Kodansha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}} - Link to Asahi Shimbun "Kotobank." == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tenno-no-mori Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] - Kyoto City Information Center [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nishikyō-ku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] revision.text:
{{nihongo|{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|qq=}}|[[:ja:天皇の杜古墳|天皇の杜古墳]]}} is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Misasagi Tsukanokoshi-chō, Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|京都市西京区御陵塚ノ越町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasagi Tsukanokoshi-chō, Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}} (as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|qq=}}). == Overview == This tumulus was constructed along the western edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|qq=}}, near the foothills on the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River (Yodo River system)|ja|桂川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura River (Yodo River system)|qq=}}. Since the medieval period, the mound has been traditionally associated with {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Montoku|qq=}}, the 55th Emperor of Japan from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}. The name "Tenno-no-mori" (lit. "Emperor's Forest") and the surrounding area name "Misasagi" (imperial mausoleum) are said to derive from this tradition. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} The mound remains well-preserved, and excavations were conducted between {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1988|qq=}} and {{ill|1989|ja|1989th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}}.Site explanation board. The tumulus has a distinctive "mirror-handle" keyhole shape with an unexpanded front portion facing south-southeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} Outer surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} and rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} (cylindrical and morning glory-shaped). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} There is no surrounding moat, but remnants of a demarcated area resembling a moat have been identified. The burial facility has not been investigated. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/4th century|qq=}} during the early {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|qq=}}. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} Archaeological evidence suggests the individual buried here was a prominent figure who controlled the Katsura River's right bank. During this period, the transition from keyhole-shaped to circular mounds is evident in the {{ill|Mukō Hills|ja|向日丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills|qq=}} tombs such as {{ill|Myōkenyama Kofun (Mukō)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōkenyama Kofun (Mukō)|qq=}} and {{ill|Den-Takabatake Tomb|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatake Tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}. However, this site stands out as a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, indicating political importance surpassing the Mukō Hills. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1922|qq=}}, and it has been developed into a public historical park. == Site History == * '''1922 (Taishō 11)''': Designated as a National Historic Site.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * '''1981-1982 (Shōwa 56-57)''': Public ownership of the surrounding area was established. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * '''1988-1989 (Shōwa 63-Heisei 1)''': Excavations were conducted. * '''1994 (Heisei 6)''': Opening of the historical park. * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': The National Historic Site "Tenno-no-mori Kofun" was integrated with other tumuli such as {{ill|Egeyama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egeyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Igenoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Otsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} into the expanded designation "Otokuni Kofun Cluster." Eight additional tumuli were included in this designation.March 1, 2016, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 31. [http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, press release, November 20, 2015). == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] (Kyoto City Information Center). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * Total length: 83.0 meters * Posterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 50.5 meters ''' Height: 7.2 meters * Anterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 33.5 meters ''' Height: 4.8 meters Tenno-no-mori Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Posterior mound top Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from posterior to anterior mound 天皇の杜古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Kyoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|京都市考古資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Archaeological Museum|qq=}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Tenno-no-mori Kofun''' (as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster) **''' Designated as a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922, with an area of 10,234 square meters. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} **''' Integrated with other tumuli in 2016 to form the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster" designation. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Site explanation board (Kyoto City Cultural Property Protection Section) * Publications by local governments ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|title=Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|publisher=Kyoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kyotofu-maibun.or.jp/data/kankou/kankou-pdf/jyouhou/kyoutofu-J71.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Information, Issue 71]}}|publisher=Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Research Center|isbn=|pages=45-46|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Information, Issue 71|1999}}}} * Other references ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 27 Kyoto City|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Haruo Wada|year=1989|chapter=Tenno-no-mori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1444522 Tenno-no-mori Kofun]|title=National Historic Site Guide|publisher=[[Kodansha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)}}}} - Link to Asahi Shimbun "Kotobank." == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tenno-no-mori Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] - Kyoto City Information Center [[JA:%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E3%81%AE%E6%9D%9C%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{translated page|ja|天皇の杜古墳|version=104551982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Cluster]] Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tenno-no-Mori Kofun]] [[ja:天皇の杜古墳]]

{{nihongo|{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|qq=}}|[[:ja:天皇の杜古墳|天皇の杜古墳]]}} is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Misasagi Tsukanokoshi-chō, Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|京都市西京区御陵塚ノ越町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasagi Tsukanokoshi-chō, Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}} (as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|qq=}}). == Overview == This tumulus was constructed along the western edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|qq=}}, near the foothills on the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River (Yodo River system)|ja|桂川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura River (Yodo River system)|qq=}}. Since the medieval period, the mound has been traditionally associated with {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Montoku|qq=}}, the 55th Emperor of Japan from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}. The name "Tenno-no-mori" (lit. "Emperor's Forest") and the surrounding area name "Misasagi" (imperial mausoleum) are said to derive from this tradition. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} The mound remains well-preserved, and excavations were conducted between {{ill|1988|ja|1988年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1988|qq=}} and {{ill|1989|ja|1989年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}}.Site explanation board. The tumulus has a distinctive "mirror-handle" keyhole shape with an unexpanded front portion facing south-southeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} Outer surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} and rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} (cylindrical and morning glory-shaped). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} There is no surrounding moat, but remnants of a demarcated area resembling a moat have been identified. The burial facility has not been investigated. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/4th century|qq=}} during the early {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|qq=}}. {{Sfn|Tenno-no-mori Kofun (National Historic Site)|ja|天皇の杜古墳（国指定史跡）}} Archaeological evidence suggests the individual buried here was a prominent figure who controlled the Katsura River's right bank. During this period, the transition from keyhole-shaped to circular mounds is evident in the {{ill|Mukō Hills|ja|向日丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills|qq=}} tombs such as {{ill|Myōkenyama Kofun (Mukō)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōkenyama Kofun (Mukō)|qq=}} and {{ill|Den-Takabatake Tomb|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatake Tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}. However, this site stands out as a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, indicating political importance surpassing the Mukō Hills. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1922|qq=}}, and it has been developed into a public historical park. == Site History == * '''1922 (Taishō 11)''': Designated as a National Historic Site.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} * '''1981-1982 (Shōwa 56-57)''': Public ownership of the surrounding area was established. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * '''1988-1989 (Shōwa 63-Heisei 1)''': Excavations were conducted. * '''1994 (Heisei 6)''': Opening of the historical park. * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': The National Historic Site "Tenno-no-mori Kofun" was integrated with other tumuli such as {{ill|Egeyama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egeyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Igenoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Otsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} into the expanded designation "Otokuni Kofun Cluster." Eight additional tumuli were included in this designation.March 1, 2016, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 31. [http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, press release, November 20, 2015). == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005646.html Tenno-no-mori Kofun] (Kyoto City Information Center). {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} * Total length: 83.0 meters * Posterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 50.5 meters ''' Height: 7.2 meters * Anterior section (two-tiered): ''' Width: 33.5 meters ''' Height: 4.8 meters Tenno-no-mori Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Posterior mound top Tenno-no-mori Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from posterior to anterior mound 天皇の杜古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Kyoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|京都市考古資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Archaeological Museum|qq=}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Tenno-no-mori Kofun''' (as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster) **''' Designated as a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922, with an area of 10,234 square meters. {{Sfn|Report on Preservation and Maintenance of Tenno-no-mori Kofun|1994|pp=5-11}} **''' Integrated with other tumuli in 2016 to form the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster" designation. ==
